title: LLMs Go Mainstream
caption: ChatGPT's chat interface, launched November 2022, reached one million users in five days.

overview.text: OpenAI launched ChatGPT on 30 November 2022, making a large language model available to the general public via a free web interface. Within five days it had one million users; within two months, 100 million — the fastest consumer-product adoption in history at the time. ChatGPT is built on GPT-3.5, a Transformer trained on vast text corpora and then fine-tuned with reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) to be helpful, harmless and honest. Its release compressed years of public and policy debate about AI into weeks.

deep.text: Large language models are Transformer decoders trained to predict the next token in a sequence, scaled to billions of parameters and trillions of tokens. Instruction-following behaviour emerges from fine-tuning on human-written prompt–response pairs (supervised fine-tuning) followed by RLHF: a reward model trained on human preference comparisons guides policy optimisation via proximal policy optimisation (PPO). The result is a model that generates fluent, contextually appropriate text across an enormous range of tasks without task-specific training.
deep.math: P(token_{t+1} | token₁ … tokenₜ)  maximised during pre-training

sources.title: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
sources.author: L. Ouyang et al. (OpenAI)
sources.publisher: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 35 (NeurIPS)
